title: Delicate Flaky Irish Potato Pie
description: A light and herbal Irish dish featuring harissa spice, this pie is a perfect representation of Ayurvedic-sattvic friendly cuisine, made with flaky crust and delicate potato filling.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the diced potatoes, harissa spice, olive oil, chopped onion, minced garlic, chopped rosemary, salt, and pepper, and toss until the potatoes are evenly coated.
In a separate large bowl, whisk together the flour and cold unsalted butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add cold water to the flour mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it out to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch pie dish and trim the edges to fit.
Fill the pie crust with the potato mixture and spread it out evenly.
Roll out the remaining dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch and use it to cover the pie.
Crimp the edges of the pie to seal it and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the pie for 45 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
